name: Eval

values.name: evalBody
values.comment: Given an EvalBodyStep, compute the next EvalBodyStep.

values.name: evalFtn
values.comment: Given an EvalFtnStep, compute the next EvalFtnStep.

values.name: evalList
values.comment: Given an ListStep, compute the next ListStep.

values.name: evalTerm
values.comment: Given an EvalTermStep, compute the next EvalTermStep.

values.name: evalTerms
values.comment: Given an EvalTermsStep, compute the next EvalTermsStep.

name: EvalStep

unions.name: BuiltInStep
unions.comment: The set of states used in defining a BuiltIn function.

unions.name: EvalBodyStep
unions.comment:
EvalBodyStep is for evaling a list of Terms, throwing away the results
from all but the last Term. State is modified along the way, as is the
namespace. Used for processing the body of a schelme function.

unions.name: EvalFtnStep
unions.comment: The set of states during eval of a schelme function.

unions.name: EvalTermStep
unions.comment:
EvalTermStep represents the possible states during eval of a
single Term. As with all the 'Steps', the state is modified during
eval, as is the namespace, although these changes may be thrown away by higher
levels of eval.

unions.name: EvalTermsStep
unions.comment:
EvalTermsStep is a set of states used to eval a list of Terms,
returning the list of resulting Terms. Mainly for processing function
arguments.
cases: { ns : EvalStep.NameSpace a, state : a, unevaledTerms : List.List (EvalStep.Term a), currentTerm : EvalStep.EvalTermStep a, evaledTerms : List.List (EvalStep.Term a) }

unions.name: ListStep
unions.comment:
The ListStep is the set of states for evaling a list Term. Evaling a list
usually results in function calls - fuctions defined in schelme, functions that
modify namespace but not state (builtin) and functions that modify namespace and
state (sideeffectors).

unions.name: SideEffectorStep
unions.comment:
The steps involved in a SideEffector function. Not all these steps
have to be used. Check out the prelude for some examples.

unions.name: Term
unions.comment: A Schelme program is a list of Terms.

aliases.name: BuiltIn
aliases.comment:
A builtin function is defined in Elm. It should process any of
the BuiltInStep states, hopefully resulting in a BuiltInFinal at the
end. BuiltInFinal doesn't include the state ('a'), so any changes
mades to state are always thrown away.

aliases.name: Function
aliases.comment:
a schelme function is a list of arg names and a list of Terms, which
are evaled sequentially when the function is called.
aliases.type: { args : List.List String.String, body : List.List (EvalStep.Term a) }

aliases.name: GlossaryEntry
aliases.comment: data structure used for making a language reference.

aliases.name: NameSpace
aliases.comment:
schelme code is always executed with a namespace, which
maps from symbol names to Terms.

aliases.name: SideEffector
aliases.comment:
A sideeffector function processes SideEffectorSteps, and should
eventually return either a SideEffectorFinal or SideEffectorError.
Unlike the BuiltIn, the SideEffector returns a modified state ('a') in
its Final step.

values.name: sxpToTerm
values.comment: parse an s-expression, yielding a single term.

values.name: sxpsToTerms
values.comment: parse a series of s-expressions, yielding a list of terms.

name: Prelude

aliases.name: BuiltInFn
aliases.type: EvalStep.NameSpace a -> a -> List.List (EvalStep.Term a) -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, EvalStep.Term a )

aliases.name: SideEffectorFn
aliases.comment: function type to pass to evalArgsSideEffector
aliases.type: EvalStep.NameSpace a -> a -> List.List (EvalStep.Term a) -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, a, EvalStep.Term a )

values.name: builtInFn
values.comment: make a BuiltIn function where arguments are NOT evaled before the BuiltInFn function is called. Useful for things like defn.

values.name: evalArgsBuiltIn
values.comment: make a BuiltIn function where arguments are evaled before the BuiltInFn function is called.

values.name: evalArgsSideEffector
values.comment: make a SideEffector function where arguments are evaled before the SideEffectorFn function is called.

values.name: math
values.comment: a NameSpace of mathy schelme functions.

values.name: mathGlossary
values.comment: Glossary of math functions

values.name: prelude
values.comment: a NameSpace of fundamental schelme functions.

values.name: preludeGlossary
values.comment: Glossary of prelude terms

name: Run

values.name: compile
values.comment: parse a string, emitting a series of Terms, which will hopefully be a valid schelme program.

values.name: evalBodyLimit
values.comment:
The way to go for incremental execution. Given an EvalBodyStep (the normal top
level of a running schelme program), execute it up to /count/ evals, returning the
last EvalBodyStep state.

values.name: run
values.comment:
given a namespace (for instance Prelude.prelude) and a state (see examples), and a schelme program,
run the program to completion, emitting an updated namespace, state, and final Term
values.type: EvalStep.NameSpace a -> a -> List.List (EvalStep.Term a) -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, a, EvalStep.Term a )

values.name: runBody
values.comment: starting with an EvalBodyStep, run to completion
values.type: EvalStep.EvalBodyStep a -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, a, EvalStep.Term a )

values.name: runBodyCount
values.comment: continue execution of an EvalBodyStep, returning the final Term and number of evals used.
values.type: EvalStep.EvalBodyStep a -> Basics.Int -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, a, ( Basics.Int, EvalStep.Term a ) )

values.name: runBodyLimit
values.comment: run a schelme EvalBodyStep with a max number of evals, erroring out if the max is reached.
values.type: EvalStep.EvalBodyStep a -> Basics.Int -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, a, ( Basics.Int, EvalStep.Term a ) )

values.name: runCount
values.comment: run a schelme program, emitting the usual products but also the number of evals taken
values.type: EvalStep.NameSpace a -> a -> List.List (EvalStep.Term a) -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, a, ( Basics.Int, EvalStep.Term a ) )

values.name: runLimit
values.comment: run a schelme program with a max number of evals, erroring out if the max is reached.
values.type: EvalStep.NameSpace a -> a -> Basics.Int -> List.List (EvalStep.Term a) -> Result.Result String.String ( EvalStep.NameSpace a, a, ( Basics.Int, EvalStep.Term a ) )

name: SExpression

unions.name: Sxp
unions.comment: S expression - either a string or a list of s expressions.

values.name: sList
values.comment: a list is a series of space separated terms surrounded by parens.

values.name: sSxp
values.comment: parse an s-expression - either a term or a list.

values.name: sSxps
values.comment: parse a series of whitespace separated terms.

values.name: sTerm
values.comment: parse an individual s-expression term (not a list).

values.name: showSxp
values.comment: print an s-expression for debug
